subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Let the masses of the three parts be m', m' and 2m'.

Momentum of first part p1=m'×30=30m' and momentum of second part p2=m'×40=40m'.

Third part will fly in a direction opposite to the direction of resultant of p1 and p2, so that total momentum of the system after explosion remains zero.

Momentum of the third part is p3=p12+p22=30m'2+40m'2=50m'

Velocity of third part, v=50m'2m'=25 m s-1

subject: Physics

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Velocity of the centre of mass is given by:

$\vec{v}_{cm} = \dfrac{m_1\vec{v}_1 + m_2\vec{v}_2}{m_1 + m_2}$

Since $m_1 = m_2 = m$, we get:

$\vec{v}_{cm} = \dfrac{m(4\hat{i}) + m(4\hat{j})}{2m} = 2\hat{i} + 2\hat{j}$ m/s

Acceleration of the centre of mass is given by:

$\vec{a}_{cm} = \dfrac{m_1\vec{a}_1 + m_2\vec{a}_2}{m_1 + m_2}$

$\vec{a}_{cm} = \dfrac{m(6\hat{i} + 6\hat{j}) + 0}{2m} = 3\hat{i} + 3\hat{j}$ m/s$^2$

Here, $\vec{v}_{cm} = 2(\hat{i} + \hat{j})$ and $\vec{a}_{cm} = 3(\hat{i} + \hat{j})$.

Since the initial velocity and acceleration of the centre of mass are parallel to each other, the centre of mass moves in a straight line path.

Answer: straight line

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
After collision,

Applying momentum conservation,

mu=mv1+5mv2  ⇒u=v1+5v2   ...1 

Coefficient of restitution, e=1=v2-v1u

∴ u=v2-v1   ...2

Adding  2u=6v2  ⇒v2=u3 and  u=u3-v1

∴ v1=u3-u=-2u3

Percentage change in kinetic energy =12mv12-12mu212mu2×100

=v12-u2u2×100=4u29-u2u2×100  =-59×100  =55.6%

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
By conservation of linear momentum,

P→i=P→f

Where P→i and P→f are initial and final momentum of system (boy + man)

Now,  0=mbv→b+mmv→m......(i)

vb= velocity of boy w.r.t. ground

vm= velocity of man w.r.t. ground

v→bm= velocity of boy w.r.t. man

So, v→bm=v→b-v→m

or v→b=v→bm+v→m

or v→b=0.7+v→m ........(ii)

From equation (i) and (ii),

0=200.7+v→m+50 v→m

v→m=-1470=-0.20 m s-1

Negative sign shows velocity of man is opposite to boy.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Given the position vector $\vec{r} = 10t^2\hat{i} + 5t^3\hat{j}$

Velocity $\vec{v} = \dfrac{d\vec{r}}{dt} = 20t\hat{i} + 15t^2\hat{j}$

Acceleration $\vec{a} = \dfrac{d\vec{v}}{dt} = 20\hat{i} + 30t\hat{j}$

At $t = 1$ s:
$\vec{r} = 10\hat{i} + 5\hat{j}$ m
$\vec{v} = 20\hat{i} + 15\hat{j}$ m/s
$\vec{a} = 20\hat{i} + 30\hat{j}$ m/s$^2$

Linear momentum $\vec{p} = m\vec{v} = 0.1(20\hat{i} + 15\hat{j}) = 2\hat{i} + 1.5\hat{j}$ kg$\cdot$m/s (Statement A is correct)

Force $\vec{F} = m\vec{a} = 0.1(20\hat{i} + 30\hat{j}) = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j}$ N (Statement B is correct)

Angular momentum $\vec{L} = \vec{r} \times \vec{p} = (10\hat{i} + 5\hat{j}) \times (2\hat{i} + 1.5\hat{j}) = (10 \times 1.5 - 5 \times 2)\hat{k} = 5\hat{k}$ J$\cdot$s (Statement C is incorrect)

Torque $\vec{\tau} = \vec{r} \times \vec{F} = (10\hat{i} + 5\hat{j}) \times (2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j}) = (10 \times 3 - 5 \times 2)\hat{k} = 20\hat{k}$ N$\cdot$m (Statement D is correct)

Therefore, statements A, B, and D are correct.

Answer: A, B and D only

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Impulse J=0.2 N s

⇒J=∫Fdt=0.2 N s

Now, angular impulse (M→) will be

Mc=∫τdt

=∫FL2dt

=L2∫Fdt=L2×J

=0.32×0.2

=0.03

Moment of inertia of the rod about the centre of mass, Icm=ML212=2×(0.3)212=0.096

Angular impulse will be equal to the change in angular momentum.

M=Icmωf-ωi

⇒0.03=0.096ωf

⇒ωf=2 rad s-1

For angular displacement, we can write θ=ωt

⇒t=θω=π2×2=π4 s.

Therefore, x=4.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
From mass ratio 2:2:3 with total mass 14 kg: individual masses are 4 kg, 4 kg, and 6 kg.
Setting coordinate axes along the motion directions of the two equal fragments: fragment 1 has momentum $\vec{p_1} = 4 \times 18\hat{i} = 72\hat{i}$ kg⋅m/s, and fragment 2 has $\vec{p_2} = 4 \times 18\hat{j} = 72\hat{j}$ kg⋅m/s.
By momentum conservation, initially at rest: $\vec{p_1} + \vec{p_2} + \vec{p_3} = 0$, so $\vec{p_3} = -72\hat{i} - 72\hat{j}$ kg⋅m/s.
Velocity of 6 kg fragment: $\vec{v_3} = \frac{\vec{p_3}}{6} = -12\hat{i} - 12\hat{j}$ m/s.
Speed: $|\vec{v_3}| = \sqrt{144 + 144} = \sqrt{288} = 12\sqrt{2}$ m/s.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Let the mass of solid cylinder is M and angle with vertical is θ. Now, we take a small element of thickness dy at a distance y from the vertex. The radius of small element is r, as shown in the figure below.

Then, tanθ=Rh=ry which will give, r=ytanθ. The density of the cone is given by,

ρ=M13πR2h=3MπR2h.

The mass of the small element is given by,

dM=ρdV

dM=3MπR2h×πr2dy=3My2tan2θR2hdy

The center of mass of the cone is given by,

z0=1M∫0hydM

z0=1M∫0hy·3My2tan2θR2hdy

z0=3R2hRh2y440h

z0=3h4.

chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
$\mathrm{m}_1=\sigma \times 5=10 \mathrm{Kg}$

$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{x}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{x}_2=\mathrm{m}_3 \mathrm{x}_3 \\ & 10 \mathrm{x}_1+2(1.5)=12(1.5) \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1=1.5 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{y}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{y}_2=\mathrm{m}_3 \mathrm{y}_3 \\ & 10 \mathrm{y}_1+2(1.5)=12 \times 1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{y}_1=0.9 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{x}_1}{\mathrm{y}_1}=\frac{1.5}{0.9}=\frac{15}{9} \\ & \mathrm{n}=15\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Kinetic energy $(K)=\frac{P^2}{2 m}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{P}=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{mK}}$
If $K_f=36 K_i$ So, $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}}=6 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}$ $\begin{aligned} \% \text { increase in momentum } & =\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}}-\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}}{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}} \times 100 \% \\ & =\frac{6 \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}}{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}} \times 100 \% \\ & =500 \% \end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
From the law of conservation of momentum we know that, $$ \mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{u}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{u}_2+\ldots .=\mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{v}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{v}_2+\ldots . $$ Given $\mathrm{m}_1=\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{m}_2=2 \mathrm{~m}$ and $\mathrm{m}_3=3 \mathrm{~m}$ and $\mathrm{u}_1=3 \mathrm{u}, \mathrm{u}_2=2 \mathrm{u}$ and $\mathrm{u}_3=\mathrm{u}$ Let the velocity when they stick $=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}$ $$ \text { Then, according to question, } $$
$$ \begin{aligned} &m \times 3 u(\hat{i})+2 m \times 2 u\left(-\hat{i} \cos 60^{\circ}-\hat{j} \sin 60^{\circ}\right) \\ &+3 \mathrm{~m} \times \mathrm{u}\left(-\hat{\mathrm{i}} \cos 60^{\circ}+\hat{\mathrm{j}} \sin 60^{\circ}\right) \\ &=(\mathrm{m}+2 \mathrm{~m}+3 \mathrm{~m}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}} \\ &\Rightarrow 3 m u \hat{\mathrm{i}}-4 \mathrm{mu} \frac{\hat{\mathrm{i}}}{2}-4 \mathrm{mu}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \hat{\mathrm{j}}\right)-3 \mathrm{mu} \frac{\hat{\mathrm{i}}}{2} \\ &+3 m u\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \hat{\mathrm{j}}\right)=6 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{v} \\ &\Rightarrow m u \hat{i}-\frac{3}{2} m u \hat{i}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} m \hat{j}=6 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{v} \\ &\Rightarrow-\frac{1}{2} m u \hat{i}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} m u \hat{j}=6 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{v} \\ &\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}=\frac{\mathrm{u}}{12}(-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\sqrt{3} \hat{\mathrm{j}}) \\ & \end{aligned} $$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Initial position of centre of mass: xCM=100+30×x40=3x4

Final position of the centre of mass: x'CM=106+30×x-y40=6+3x-y4

As position of centre of mass remains same,

xCM=x'CM ⇒3x4=6+3x-y4⇒3x=6+3x-3y  ⇒y=2 cm

Alternate method, ∆xCOM=m1∆x1+m2∆x2m1+m2⇒0=10×6+30y⇒y=-2 cm
